heading: Hospice program survey and enforcement procedures
label: 1395i–6 Hospice program survey and enforcement procedures
notes: References in TextSection 3(a)(2) of the Improving Medicare Post-Acute Care Transformation Act of 2014, referred to in subsec. (a)(5), means , , , which is not classified to the Code.section 3(a)(2) of Pub. L. 113–185128 Stat. 1968Oct. 6, 2014
amendments: References in TextSection 3(a)(2) of the Improving Medicare Post-Acute Care Transformation Act of 2014, referred to in subsec. (a)(5), means , , , which is not classified to the Code.section 3(a)(2) of Pub. L. 113–185128 Stat. 1968Oct. 6, 2014

children.heading: Frequency
children.label: 1 Frequency
children.text: Any entity that is certified as a hospice program (as defined in ) shall be subject to a standard survey by an appropriate State or local survey agency, or an approved accreditation agency, as determined by the Secretary, not less frequently than once every 36 months.section 1395x(dd)(2) of this title

children.heading: Public transparency of survey and certification information
children.label: 2 Public transparency of survey and certification information

children.heading: Submission of information to the Secretary
children.label: A Submission of information to the Secretary

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each State or local survey agency, and each national accreditation body with respect to which the Secretary has made a finding under respecting the accreditation of a hospice program by such body, shall submit, in a form and manner, and at a time, specified by the Secretary for purposes of this paragraph, information respecting any survey or certification made with respect to a hospice program by such survey agency or body, as applicable. Such information shall include any inspection report made by such survey agency or body with respect to such survey or certification, any enforcement actions taken as a result of such survey or certification, and any other information determined appropriate by the Secretary.section 1395bb(a) of this title

children.heading: Required inclusion of specified form
children.label: ii Required inclusion of specified form
children.text: With respect to a survey under this subsection carried out by a national accreditation body described in clause (i) on or after , information described in such clause shall include Form CMS-2567 (or a successor form), along with such additional information determined appropriate by such body.October 1, 2021

children.heading: Public disclosure of information
children.label: B Public disclosure of information
children.text: Beginning not later than , the Secretary shall publish the information submitted under subparagraph (A) on the public website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services in a manner that is prominent, easily accessible, readily understandable, and searchable. The Secretary shall provide for the timely update of such information so published.October 1, 2022

children.heading: Consistency of surveys
children.label: 3 Consistency of surveys
children.text: Each State and the Secretary shall implement programs to measure and reduce inconsistency in the application of survey results among surveyors.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a survey conducted under this subsection on or after , by more than 1 individual, such survey shall be conducted by a multidisciplinary team of professionals (including a registered professional nurse).October 1, 2021

children.heading: Prohibition of conflicts of interest
children.label: B Prohibition of conflicts of interest
children.text: Beginning , a State may not use as a member of a survey team under this subsection an individual who is serving (or has served within the previous 2 years) as a member of the staff of, or as a consultant to, the program surveyed respecting compliance with the requirements of or who has a personal or familial financial interest in the program being surveyed.October 1, 2021section 1395x(dd) of this title

children.heading: Training
children.label: C Training
children.text: The Secretary shall provide, not later than , for the comprehensive training of State and Federal surveyors, and any surveyor employed by a national accreditation body described in paragraph (2)(A)(i), in the conduct of surveys under this subsection, including training with respect to the review of written plans for providing hospice care (as described in ). No individual shall serve as a member of a survey team with respect to a survey conducted on or after such date unless the individual has successfully completed a training and testing program in survey and certification techniques that has been approved by the Secretary.October 1, 2021section 1395f(a)(7)(B) of this title

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 5 Funding
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund under to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account, of $10,000,000 for each fiscal year (beginning with fiscal year 2022) for purposes of carrying out this subsection and subsection (b). Sums so transferred shall remain available until expended. Any transfer pursuant to this paragraph shall be in addition to any transfer pursuant to section 3(a)(2) of the Improving Medicare Post-Acute Care Transformation Act of 2014.section 1395i of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a special focus program for enforcement of requirements for hospice programs that the Secretary has identified as having substantially failed to meet applicable requirements of this chapter.

children.heading: Periodic surveys
children.label: 2 Periodic surveys
children.text: Under such special focus program, the Secretary shall conduct surveys of each hospice program in the special focus program not less than once every 6 months.

children.heading: Situations involving immediate jeopardy
children.label: 1 Situations involving immediate jeopardy
children.text: If the Secretary determines on the basis of a standard survey or otherwise that a hospice program that is certified for participation under this subchapter is no longer in compliance with the requirements specified in and determines that the deficiencies involved immediately jeopardize the health and safety of the individuals to whom the program furnishes items and services, the Secretary shall take immediate action to ensure the removal of the jeopardy and correction of the deficiencies or terminate the certification of the program, and may provide, in addition, for 1 or more of the other remedies described in paragraph (5)(B).section 1395x(dd) of this title

children.heading: Situations not involving immediate jeopardy
children.label: 2 Situations not involving immediate jeopardy
children.text: If the Secretary determines on the basis of a standard survey or otherwise that a hospice program that is certified for participation under this subchapter is no longer in compliance with the requirements specified in and determines that the deficiencies involved do not immediately jeopardize the health and safety of the individuals to whom the program furnishes items and services, the Secretary may (for a period not to exceed 6 months) impose remedies developed pursuant to paragraph (5)(A), in lieu of terminating the certification of the program. If, after such a period of remedies, the program is still no longer in compliance with such requirements, the Secretary shall terminate the certification of the program.section 1395x(dd) of this title

children.heading: Penalty for previous noncompliance
children.label: 3 Penalty for previous noncompliance
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a hospice program that is certified for participation under this subchapter is in compliance with the requirements specified in but, as of a previous period, did not meet such requirements, the Secretary may provide for a civil money penalty under paragraph (5)(B)(i) for the days in which the Secretary finds that the program was not in compliance with such requirements.section 1395x(dd) of this title

children.heading: Option to continue payments for noncompliant hospice programs
children.label: 4 Option to continue payments for noncompliant hospice programs
A
children.text: the State or local survey agency finds that it is more appropriate to take alternative action to assure compliance of the program with such requirements than to terminate the certification of the program;

children.label: B
children.text: the program has submitted a plan and timetable for corrective action to the Secretary for approval and the Secretary approves the plan of corrective action; and

children.label: C
children.text: the program agrees to repay to the Federal Government payments received under this subchapter during such period if the corrective action is not taken in accordance with the approved plan and timetable.

children.label: I
children.text: a range of remedies to apply to hospice programs under the conditions described in paragraphs (1) through (4); and

children.label: II
children.text: appropriate procedures for appealing determinations relating to the imposition of such remedies.

children.heading: Conditions of imposition of remedies
children.label: ii Conditions of imposition of remedies
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall develop and implement specific procedures with respect to the conditions under which each of the remedies developed under clause (i) is to be applied, including the amount of any fines and the severity of each of these remedies. Such procedures shall be designed so as to minimize the time between identification of deficiencies and imposition of these remedies and shall provide for the imposition of incrementally more severe fines for repeated or uncorrected deficiencies.October 1, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: Civil money penalties in an amount not to exceed $10,000 for each day of noncompliance by a hospice program with the requirements specified in .section 1395x(dd) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Suspension of all or part of the payments to which a hospice program would otherwise be entitled under this subchapter with respect to items and services furnished by a hospice program on or after the date on which the Secretary determines that remedies should be imposed pursuant to paragraphs (1) and (2).

children.label: iii
children.text: The appointment of temporary management to oversee the operation of the hospice program and to protect and assure the health and safety of the individuals under the care of the program while improvements are made in order to bring the program into compliance with all such requirements.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Subject to subclause (II), the provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this subsection in the same manner as such provisions apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: Retention of amounts for hospice program improvements
children.label: II Retention of amounts for hospice program improvements
children.text: The Secretary may provide that any portion of civil money penalties collected under this subsection may be used to support activities that benefit individuals receiving hospice care, including education and training programs to ensure hospice program compliance with the requirements of .section 1395x(dd) of this title

children.heading: Suspension of payment
children.label: ii Suspension of payment
children.text: A finding to suspend payment under subparagraph (B)(ii) shall terminate when the Secretary finds that the program is in substantial compliance with all requirements of .section 1395x(dd) of this title

children.heading: Temporary management
children.label: iii Temporary management
children.text: The temporary management under subparagraph (B)(iii) shall not be terminated until the Secretary has determined that the program has the management capability to ensure continued compliance with all the requirements referred to in such subparagraph.

children.heading: Relationship to other remedies
children.label: D Relationship to other remedies
children.text: The remedies developed under subparagraph (A) are in addition to sanctions otherwise available under State or Federal law and shall not be construed as limiting other remedies, including any remedy available to an individual at common law.
